summary:
The “needle” was a memory-ordering bug: producer and consumer used relaxed atomics, which is unsafe on weak-ordering CPUs (e.g., ARM).  
Fix applied:

ring_buffer.c
1. Producer
   • load read_idx with memory_order_acquire  
   • publish write_idx with memory_order_release
2. Consumer
   • load write_idx and read_idx with memory_order_acquire  
   • publish read_idx with memory_order_release

These acquire/release pairs guarantee the data in slots[] becomes visible before the indices are seen by the opposite thread.

Running ./test.sh now passes:

PASS: All tests passed — no races, correct output
